revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八坂神社 (飯能市山手町)]]
{{infobox religious building | name = Hanno Yasaka Shrine | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} | image = File:Yasaka Jinja (Yamatecho, Hanno).JPG | image description = Hanno Yasaka Shrine Entrance | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} {{ill|Hannō|en|Hannō|qid=Q850472|lt=Ino City|1=Hannō}}, Yamate-cho, 4 (address) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.855897|N|139.31415|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | main_hall = | alternate_names = Also called “Hanno Yasaka Shrine” and “Otennō-sama” | festival = The festival is held on the Saturday and Sunday of the main festival period. | map = | location = {{ill|Hannō|qid=Q850472|1=Hannō}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}) in Yamate-cho, Ino City is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yamate-cho, Ino City, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is also known as “Ino Yasaka Shrine.”
== Deity == The primary deity enshrined is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == Its date of founding is unknown. According to the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}, the shrine’s establishment history is not recorded. In the past it was known as Setaka Gion-gu. Its name was changed to Yasaka Shrine in the early Meiji period due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}}.
== Grounds == A notable feature on the grounds is a stone mound called “Nakazato Fuji,” which resembles Mt. Fuji. This mound was built by local pilgrims (the {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|qid=Q11456201|lt=Fuji-kō|1=Fujiko (religion)}}) from the Edo period to early Showa period. A stone monument dated to 1822 suggests its construction began in the Edo period and was completed in the early Meiji period.
The ema hall contains several votive plaques and pilgrimage scrolls from the Edo period which depict the shrine’s historical landscape.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ino Festival|ja|飯能まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ino Festival|1=Q11666657|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666657}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.hanno.saitama.jp/info/prev.asp?fol_id=2168 Information on Yasaka Shrine] (Ino City official website)
{{shinto-stub}}
